ocr.text: In the dynamic landscape of professional and business services, understanding the financial performance of consulting firms is
essential for strategic planning and resource allocation. The correlation between revenue generation and consultant engagement
is particularly significant, as firms strive to optimize their workforce efficiency while maintaining high service quality. The
accompanying table, "Revenue per Consultant," provides a comprehensive overview of key metrics that illustrate the financial
health and operational effectiveness of Capgemini's consulting division in North America. This analysis encompasses various
consultant categories, including part-time consultants and contractors, reflecting the diverse workforce structure that

characterizes the sector.

One of the critical metrics captured in this table is the revenue generated per consultant, which is
pivotal in assessing the firm's profitability and operational capacity. As firms adopt flexible work arrangements, including part-
time roles and contractor engagements, it becomes increasingly important to analyze how these arrangements impact overall
revenue. The data indicates that part-time consultants contribute significantly to the firm's revenue streams, with average
revenues reported at $200K per consultant. This figure underscores the potential of part-time roles in enhancing operational

efficiency while meeting client demands.

Furthermore, the table also delineates the total revenue figures, amounting to $20
million, which provides insight into the scale and scope of Capgemini's consulting operations within the region. By examining
this aggregate revenue alongside the performance metrics of individual consultants, stakeholders can gain a clearer
understanding of the firm’s financial dynamics.
ConsultantType  Headcount  Revenue  RevenueperConsultant
Full-Time  500  $100M  $200K
Part-Time  100  $20M  $200K  Contractor
$150M  $187.5K  GrowthRate  10%  ConsultantUtilization
80%  AverageProjectSize  $500K  ProjectsCompleted  150
ClientSatisfaction  90%  NewClients  25  RepeatClients
50  MarketShare  20%  InvestmentinTraining  $5M
The balance between full-time and part-time consultants, as well as the strategic use of contractors, plays a crucial role in
shaping revenue outcomes. In an era where agility and responsiveness to market demands are paramount, the ability to leverage

a flexible workforce can serve as a competitive advantage.

Ultimately, this analysis not only highlights the revenue
contributions of various consultant categories but also serves as a foundation for future strategic initiatives aimed at optimizing
workforce deployment. By continuing to monitor these metrics, Capgemini can enhance its operational strategies, ensuring
sustained growth and profitability in the competitive North American consulting landscape. As firms navigate the complexities
of the modern business environment, the insights derived from this revenue per consultant analysis will be instrumental in
informing decision-making processes that drive long-term success.
